message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should pay full reimbursement when damaged item is made of dragon material (overrides 50% clause)"
Expected: payout 900 for a dragon sword with enchantment 9 damaged 1000 (full reimbursement, then -100 deductible)
Current error: Expected 900, received 400
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Note: If item.material === "dragon", full reimbursement (1.0 rate), overriding the 50% enchantment clause.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.
I need to add minimal logic for the dragon material override. The reduced reimbursement check should not apply when the material is "dragon".

input.old_string: type Item = { type: string; cursed?: boolean; enchantment?: number };
input.new_string: type Item = { type: string; cursed?: boolean; enchantment?: number; material?: string };

content.text:
All tests pass (28 passing, 7 todo).

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added a check in `hasReducedReimbursement` so that if `item.material === "dragon"`, the function returns false (no reduction). Also extended the `Item` type to include an optional `material` property.
**Result**: All tests now pass (28 passing)
**Approach**: Minimal — added a single-line early return in the existing `hasReducedReimbursement` function. This is the simplest possible solution: just override the reduced reimbursement check when dragon material is present.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
